KatLikesTacos 6/06/2022 1:27 PM
I'll give some drops feedback that's semi-related. I've gotten a LOT of grumbling the last few rounds of drops from the community over set recolors. The Dawn and Twilight Hunter sets have quite a few of my viewers... Disenchanted with the entire idea of drops, pretty much. So if there's going to be more drops offered, I feel like that criticism is only going to get louder if there isn't a change to what items are dropped, or if sets aren't being completed in a somewhat prompt time frame. As to which method, I'm personally torn. Cumulative won't benefit my growth during the drops period, I think a longer watch time requirement will stress viewers out more, even if the reward is greater. So if someone is out of town and unable to access internet 4 out of 5 drops days, they're now missing 8 cosmetics total, instead of being able to binge them all the last day. Both systems have pros and cons, I think Phuzzy's idea of Community feedback is brilliant.

1:37 PM

1:39 PM

Spammals 6/06/2022 2:36 PM
I'm abstaining from the vote but will give my 2 cents short form Option 1 is like how we do drops already but XL. I like the idea of increased watch time but worry the increased items will put pressure on Rare to make more items. Option 2. I worry for the smaller streamers like myself, and American streamers. jjgaymer☠ 6/06/2022 2:37 PM
There's a couple things I would probably add to this to help out things around drops into perspective. For the actual drops items themselves, although you might hear sentiment from viewers that they might not like recolours and would prefer new sets etc we don't actually see any evidence that new/unique sets perform any better than recolours when it gets to the numbers. So while I get that everyone would always want the shiniest and best thing we have we have to look at the value add there. Do we see more viewers when we put on new/unique sets? Not that I can see from the data. There seems to much stronger indicators of what effects viewer numbers like: The game is performing well at the moment or the time of year is Holiday/Spring. Then when it comes to the numbers of cosemtics we create. We don't just have an unlimited bank of cosmetics to give away as again, these do cost quite a considerable sum of money to create (even if they are recolours). So when we do look at how we distribute them, double the cosmetics per drops period does mean that we would be halving the frequency pretty much. I think we have to find a nice balance here. I do get the argument about fatigue on sets though so what we might see going forward is less "full" sets and more of just the "clothing" or "ship" for a particular set.
Spammals 6/06/2022 2:42 PM
I'd rather not have 50% less drops and I think it will trigger people getting random parts of sets rather than complete sets. Option 3: Drops as they are now but increase the view time lol
HitboTC 6/06/2022 3:06 PM
Thanks @jjgaymer☠ for the feedback! Honestly I would love to try something different.

NateaCuiki 6/06/2022 5:27 PM
I don't know if it's possible, but, I have an idea mix between how it works now & 2: Option 3: Cumulative but only 1 item unblock a day. For example - 4 days with drops, how it works now. Friday, Saturday, Sunday and Monday. Friday start with 1 item from Drops (pants), watch 20 min to get them. Saturday new item unblocks (shirt) + Friday item... watch 20 to get first item (if I can't get them the past day) and another 20 min to obtain second. Sunday again new item unblocks, this day I can get today item and 2 past... Monday latest day, unblocks new item and I can get todays item and if I missing any, cumulative time for past items. This system it's how it works now, but cumulative advantage if viewer miss past days. Sorry for my bad english xD (edited)
Captain Falcore 6/06/2022 6:06 PM
Option 2 for me, as evidence has shown cumulative drops do not work in the sense of people thinking "Oh I'll just get all mine from this streamer now, and then not bother with anyone else" I know this not to be the case, both as a viewer, and a person involved in such drops. People are worried that person A who streams and 10am will get the majority of the views rather than person B who streams at 4pm. But the reality of the situation is, there is no RUSH to get drops like this. Think about it from your perspective "Should I watch this person who i don't really know that well stream now for 4 hours, or swing by later on when my fave streamer is on". Cumulative drops better stretch out the viewership in my experience, as people are far more chilled out about getting the drops in their own time.
3

HitboTC 7/06/2022 2:07 PM

KroTukk 7/06/2022 2:12 PM
Cumulative, I think, only has one downside. The larger 'point of no return'.. beyond that, its a much better experience than missing two items bc your pet fish needed to go to the groomers or whatever reason someone might miss a day.
2
Captain Falcore 7/06/2022 2:13 PM
Cumulative honestly is a great approach for everyone. You have to think about it from your perspective as a viewer. The way that it currently stands is If a drop is on, on that day, and you are most likely working. Of course you are going to click on the first partner you see, either before, or during work, watch you're 20 minutes, and rinse and repeat. This is why we see the majority of views being handed out to the streamers who very first start streaming. With cumulative drops, people are not bound to a time nor day, there is no RUSH to get those drops, and what happens is, people watch the streamer of their choice, at their time, at their own pace. The theory of "First person on gets the views" is infact the reality for the current system NOT cumulative drops.
3
2:16 PM
I mean sure, there will be SOME people who AFK a streamer and just get them all in one day. But that's not a viewer you want "watching" anyway. Twitch Drops are all about promoting both the streamer, and the game they are showcasing, it's never about how many views you get, Twitch is NEVER about views. It's about getting people to stick around, and Drops give you that chance to show people you're the type of person they want in their entertainment lives. It's not about trying to stack the most views in the hopes it'll somehow get you more recognition!
2

PhuzzyBond 25/06/2022 9:48 AM
Got an odd idea for SoT Twitch Drops, i have been doing Albion Online drops recently, most streamers have 10-20K followers are sitting on 4K viewers. which is much higher than we get in percentage even though SoT has a healthier consistent player base and views on Twitch (without drops). Albion Online Drops are basically Different rarity chests that you get and open ingame in a cumulative order. Imagine we have Castaway, Seafarer, Marauder and Captain Chests as drops, you get that chest as a drop, but then you open it in a UI ingame and that gets you random cosmetic. However, if you already have that cosmetic then it rewards you with Dobloons Gold, or some ancient coins instead. The unknown factor of drops and trying your luck seems like a very effective way to get these, specially if they know that hidden somewhere in there an Ebon Flintlock or some Onyx item . This might also ease of the needed work to keep creating items every single drops. Just have a pool of drops items with different rarities and chances that maybe gets updated with more items whenever you feel like it, it will also give players a chance to get older cosmetics if you add all older drops in there, including Ori Shipset. I guarantee you 3-4x the views for everyone in a system like this. Which eventually pushes Sea of Thieves towards the top of views on Twitch (edited)
lirolake [SoT Fest] 25/06/2022 10:04 AM
I remember in the past Rare has always stated that they want the player to know exactly what they will be getting. This might have only applied to the pirate emporium, but I feel like it's been taken onboard as a game-wide thing
PhuzzyBond 25/06/2022 10:07 AM
Yeah , I feel like the only downside of this is the pre-decision of knowing what you're getting, "not interested in this set" so they just won't watch, leading to lower views. On the backend this could make it much easier to create cosmetics for drops and each announcement will just be the same "drops coming" without what type since it's all in chests. As a pirate game, getting them in the form of treasure also feels more relatable
DRUMZA! 25/06/2022 12:22 PM
Unless a different and more remarkable drop set comes, I think that we will not be able to capture the interest and relevance in the publications with these drops. And I beg you. To reflect the feedback from my community, they no longer want their old-fashioned cosmetics. We need to fill the drops with more diverse and more eye-catching cosmetics. I hope there is such a plan and it will be with us in the future. As I said, I always respect what has been decided, I just wanted to present the criticism that my community has reflected on me. Regards, love..

BriittyBear 27/07/2022 8:55 PM
I just want to double check what is dropping each day in this upcoming set! August 5th - RM Jacket August 6th - RM Battle Hat August 7th - RM Trousers I believe this is the correct order according to the post in #announcements, but just want to confirm!
lirolake [SoT Fest] 27/07/2022 9:31 PM
@BriittyBear The drops will be working slightly different this time around. All 3 will be available on the 5th, but they'll need to watch for 1 hour to earn the jacket. Then another hour for the hat. Then another hour for the trousers. They can watch for 3 hours in 1 session, or they can watch for 3 hours in multiple sessions over the few days they're active (edited)
BriittyBear 27/07/2022 9:32 PM
AHHH duh! Silly blonde moment! Thank you VERY much!! You rock!!

🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 27/07/2022 10:37 PM

jjgaymer☠ 28/07/2022 11:03 AM
@HitboTC I think there are a few things we want to look out for. 1. That we see around the same or more drops claimed as we usually do ( I understand this set has already been given out so don't want to index on that too much). 2. That the overall sentiment from the community feels positive to this new way of doing it. i.e. Does it fix the issue with people feeling like they missed out on certain drops? 3. I would like to see a relatively even distribution of drops across the three days and not a huge spike in the first few hours then a massive decline.
2

Dedro 7/08/2022 5:07 PM
Little feedback for the drops from a little streamer. I did stream yesterday and today, and the numbers where way more high then normal drops days. mads 8/08/2022 2:10 AM
Even from a non-streamer perspective, this format of drops is brilliant. Allowing people the chance to catch all the drops on one day and not having to tell them “tough luck” if they miss a day for whatever reason is great. Yes, the numbers have also been insane for us, but I think that’s in part due to the new season being so awesome. Even if numbers hadn’t changed at all, the positive community feedback is undeniable!
Sandromeda 8/08/2022 8:27 PM
lots of people loved the new drops system, and looking at just a glance the community was booming. The numbers of viewers I had was the highest its ever been during a drops session. Overall extremely positive outcome! Please continue this new change ❤️
Sandromeda
lots of people loved the new drops system, and looking at just a glance the community was booming. The numbers of viewers I had was the highest its ever been during a drops session. Overall extremely positive outcome! Please continue this new change ❤️
PoisonHearrt 8/08/2022 10:48 PM
Same happened to me along w/ more people talking, thanking for the drops each day & participating in other things on the stream while waiting for the drops/sticking around for the 6 pack giveaway instead of dropping off & checking back in every few hours in the dozens until the giveaway happened. It was nice to meet a bunch of new community members & most were very positive individuals. Didn't need raids to keep the love flowing at a way higher degree of support. It was refreshing & beautiful.
PoisonHearrt 8/08/2022 11:14 PM
I forgot to mention because it was drops to get all in 1 day. No pressure of streaming for what ever period of time. This way like @Captain Falcore had mentioned that he has Sundays off. If something comes up for any of us or being sick, no one in the Community is missing a drop & have multi-days to get them all so taking that stress off of us to stream every day of drops no matter what, is now a thing of the past. ️ TY all for recommending other ways & for the team to try new approaches on delivering the goods.
ShockwaveZ3R0 9/08/2022 12:27 AM
Yeah it seems almost completely unanimous that this system is better for everyone and should be kept this way permanently.

HitboTC 9/08/2022 12:58 AM
I really hope this new system sticks around.
DRUMZA! 9/08/2022 8:29 AM
Thanks for the drops.
Captain Falcore 9/08/2022 1:48 PM
For viewership, across the board, I was seeing some partners hitting 1k that are normally stuck at 300-400 views, and it was consistent over the course of the drops. The feedback from the chat was also positive, everyone (once they understood it) loved the fact they could tune in whenever, and didn't have to go AFK in work on a specific day to get them. The chat quality because of this was greatly improved, as people were there when they had time to watch unlike the old setup. People have been asking "Will it be like this from now on, because I love it" Resounding success, we love it, they love it, we all love it.

Was chatting to @LadyHaradaNiwa and she said it would be cool if we had a system like WoW where anyone who STILL needs the 6 pack can gift 2 subs to earn the 6 pack through Twitch... ties it off and gives anyone still keen to get it the chance plus some bonus sub revenue for everyone
LadyHaradaNiwa 12/12/2022 5:09 PM
Like maybe over the festive season to giving back to your viewers
Cliff 12/12/2022 5:14 PM
As somebody with a little bit of insight on the inner machinations of Blizzard, I'd recommend not taking them as an example. The whole "gifting subs for ingame loot" thing had an extremely bitter aftertaste when they first did it with Overwatch because they were having viewers shovel money into the pockets of literal millionaires (who didn't even play the game) to get those goodies. It also felt like they did that to shut the streamers up because unlike the partner programme that Rare has, Blizzard's creator relations are absolutely atrocious. Might be a bit of a biased take from me because I'm not a streamer and I definitely have a bone to pick with Blizzard so yeah, take it for what it is

Blurbs
Can we ease up on the drop frequency? I know many here will likely disagree because of the viewership boost but it's pretty exhausting from a viewer standpoint. Less frequent, more unique drops would be so so much better (as well as more drops for everyone)
mads 6/01/2023 10:49 PM
I’m with you. It’s absolutely exhausting. 10:50 PM

Captain Falcore 6/01/2023 11:28 PM
Hear me out here... Obsidian Stool emote.. Pull out an obsisian stool Sit on it. BOOM. The replacement for Obs 6 pack. BOOM, right there, done! Ship it! It's done.
HitboTC 7/01/2023 1:41 AM
Twitch Drops are great! Always a fun opportunity to meet new people.
ShockwaveZ3R0 7/01/2023 4:47 AM
Not to sound spiteful but I only see streamers that are definitely not having any trouble paying their bills asking for less drops. The majority of us aren't there yet...
2
HitboTC 7/01/2023 6:04 AM
at the end of the day Twitch Drops are an incredible opportunity that RARE gives us content creators to grow our channels. If they are too much you can always take a break or do what I do and just always take one day off from them. I remain thankful for each and every drops day! They really are game changers for meeting new people. ❤️
3
Beardageddon 7/01/2023 6:11 AM
Twitch drops are an amazing opportunity and tbh I don't have any complaints about the frequency of them, in fact quite the opposite, a lot of people the days after drops say they wish they'd been on for longer as they didn't get them all. Drops also give our channels the boost they need to increase the ad incentive offer. Concurrent viewership x hours streamed = ad offer. After 8 years of streaming the ad incentive finally gives me peace of mind that I don't need to rely on subs, as a sole earner providing for a family this is a huge weight off my shoulders. I get drops can be intense for sure, but this is part of being a partner and representing a game, as hitbo says, there is always the option to take time off. Not only this, drops means that as our viewer counts are inflated we can take extra days off later in the month for those that choose to do ad incentives as this will keep your ccv higher and give you a much needed break (edited)
3
HitboTC 7/01/2023 6:15 AM
@Beardageddon extremely well said my friend.

Mother0fChickens 8/01/2023 3:56 AM
I've been saying for a while that when partners get new drops, everyone else should get the previous drop, that way people have the chance to grab old stuff they may have missed, partners don't lose viewership because everyone wants new stuff, and non partners don't suffer as much through drops as they do now (lets not all pretend its not an issue for non partners/people pushing for partner) Hunt Showdown tend to do it in a similar way, they give everyone drops, and their partners exclusives, so the partners still get the drops perk, but it gives others a chance when viewership is being funneled in this way... Ultimately I have no issue with how drops currently function, but I do think there should be more on offer for the wider SoT streaming community in these times... I remember my partner push all too well...

PhuzzyBond 8/01/2023 6:11 AM

Beardageddon 8/01/2023 8:56 AM
This might be an unpopular opinion and I'm sorry if it is, but why exactly is partners having perks over none partners a problem for some? I get that it's hard I really do, but, the reason the push for sot partner was so hard for a lot of people was because rare were changing it up, now look how many new partners we have. At the end of the day, people having something to aspire to and work toward should be a driving force, not omg its so not fair that they get this and I don't. I'm going to do the boomer thing here, but back when I was applying for twitch partner, there was no affiliate, there was no transcoding options there was nothing, but despite being severely hindered by these things and twitch partners having a load of bonuses people still made partner. If everyone has everything partners have then what have they got to aspire to? Last point on this because I feel we bang our heads against a brick wall every time this comes up. Rare have the analytics to know what works best for drops and to be brutally honest and blunt you've only got to look at the numbers on twitch when drops aren't on to see how much difference they make. Drops frequency = higher ccv for sot as a whole which = a higher ccv for us for longer periods between drops. I guarantee you without the frequency of drops now we would all be looking at significantly lower ccv and earnings As content creators we have a finite life span, 1 day all what we have will be gone and we will have to get 9-5 jobs or find work alongside it We should be so grateful we've been given this opportunity to monetise our content so regularly and in a way that is beneficial to the product we're supposed to be representing and selling, whilst simultaneously meaning we can take breaks and afford to allow ourselves time off. I don't know all your stories, but let me tell you I've done the 7 day a week grind for around 4 years and it's something I never want to experience again (edited)
BehavingBeardly 8/01/2023 9:07 AM

flotsam 8/01/2023 9:30 AM
The last campaign was drops for everyone and it did better than any of the recent drops for partners campaigns by a mile. More people streaming the game (including a lot of non-SoT mains streaming because of drops, bringing in entirely new viewers). Way more people watching. All that's good for us longterm if SoT is our main game. Personally, and I know I'm in the tiny minority here, I'd prefer more drops for everyone campaigns (or making all drops for everyone) because it's better for the category as a whole, not trying to gatekeep viewer boosts as a partner perk (that leads to lower viewership/participation overall). Let people watch who they wanna watch. Boost the entire category. Bring in new faces who are variety streamers. All good for us longterm. And for what it's worth, unless you're going hard on ads or sponsored streams, the higher CCV doesn't necessarily correlate to higher revenue. I generally make significantly less revenue during drops compared to a normal stream, despite having inflated viewership, because I don't do sponsored streams and I try to keep my stream as ad free as possible for my community's sake.
Beardageddon 8/01/2023 9:36 AM
I commend you on keeping your stream ad free, it's something I always wanted to do and swore I never would run them, but since having a kid my priorities have changed and my community has been overwhelmingly supportive of it as I don't run ads during action, something I recommend everyone practice. Not to mention, the cost of living crisis is going to hit out pockets hard this year, we will all suffer on sub count etc, if you can afford to not run ads then more power to you. But ultimately this is our business and livelihood and I can tell you from being top of other categories on twitch then dropping down, it does not feel good, so I say embrace it while you can and earn what you can But I'd be highly surprised if he vast majority of your earnings aren't during drops weeks, that would just be mad as my analytics and I'm sure @BehavingBeardly will say the same, analytics show a massive uptick in earnings during drops periods, both pre and post advertising revenue (edited)
BehavingBeardly 8/01/2023 9:44 AM

I’ve passed because I don’t want to be dependent upon special privileges in order to make rent. That’s all beside the point tho. I’m just arguing in favor of what benefits the game and the game’s presence on twitch the most. Not what would benefit me as an individual partner.

LDSylvr 8/01/2023 2:23 PM
Here's my thoughts on this, as I have read all of your thoughts and agree with something each of you have said... We all grinded to be here in the Partner Program and, not to sound like a dick, but anything that can be exclusively obtained through drops should always be reserved for us. Plain and simple. It's the perk of being in this program. Now, I'm all about helping out the "smaller guy" and a lot of you guys know that. I go out of my way to help other people grow their channels, so when I see it's a previous drop that's happening and EVERYONE has drops for it, I'll either change up my schedule (if I can) to accommodate those people trying to grow their channel that are benefiting off of the drops and I'll stream on a different day when the drops aren't happening, or I just won't put "drops" in my stream title if I have to stream. As far as ad revenue goes, I'm with Beard and Phuzzy on this... I swore I would never run ads in my stream until the incentives came out. But I make sure that I educate people who aren't typically a sub in my channel, but are always there lurking or watching, how much Twitch Turbo benefits both them and me as well as other streamers. Like I go in depth and break it down for them and I hardly ever have people complaining about ads anymore and I still see that chunk of change coming in. Now... having said all that, everyone needs to realize and understand that not everyone has the same financial status as the person next to them. Not everyone has kids, a family, a house mortgage, etc that changes people's once stood by perspectives, so you're gonna have a lot of different opinions when it comes to something like this. As far as the "people/viewers" go... everyone is going to want something for FREE and more often of it. It's human nature, so I'm perfectly fine with how the drops are now and how frequent they are now. Does it benefit some more than others? Yes. Of course it does. The early bird gets the worm when it comes to drops. The stats show that if drops are happening, no matter if it's for Partners only or for everyone, whoever gets on first and starts stream is going to hold those numbers for the majority of the day (unless you're using a viewbot to take your view count up to 1.3k viewers like clockwork). But I will say this lastly... if you're solely relying on streaming SoT (just hitting live and playing the game) to pay your bills, you're doing it wrong. You need growth in other areas and income coming from other areas so that if you don't get the early jump on drops, you're still not taking a huge hit. And I'm sure I'll have some disagreeing with me here, and that's fine. It's okay to disagree with people. It's okay to have different views than others. That's what makes us all unique.
1

H1gh Voltage 8/01/2023 9:33 PM
Love to see lots of feedback on Twitch Drops and, like Jo said earlier, it's a great topic to discuss on our next Partner call in a few days. We plan to reveal future plans for the Drops there anyway, so I don't want to spill it all out now, but please accept this long text in response: Before Christmas break we have reviewed tons of data on how all kinds of Drops affected both partnered and non-partnered channels, our own channel and SoT category as a whole and had some discussions with our Cosmetics team. The main takeaways are:
  • Twitch Drops are doing great things for everybody (including our Social Media and website traffic), so no point in making them less frequent.
  • It's not just Drops and giveaways that drive good viewership up. Naturally, in-game content updates (Seasons, Adventures, major patches) are more significant in creating the spikes and general interest around watching and playing SoT. So if we all want SoT to perform great on Twitch, the state of the game and it's content are super important.
  • You'd be surprised, but despite all these "we want more unique drops" sentiments shouted both at you as Partners and at us at Rare... there is actually very little difference in how many players claim the Drops.
Whether it's a recolour or something unique - players claim about the same amount of items every time, with the exception of sets that were used a lot in the past and just lost their appeal over time. Creating whole new exclusive sets is simply not worth it when good quality recolours with slight geometry tweaks are performing just as well at the moment. We'll keep monitoring though to see if things change.
  • Exclusive items tied to specific Twitch live events (like the Orb) create a very intriguing area to explore for our Creator Engagement team. Hopefully we can do something about it this year. And addressing a few questions/bits of feedback from above:
  • No OG Morningstar as it is a Tall Tale set
  • We don't intend to re-use Partner Drops as everyone's Drops soon after the original release. At least until they get very old and there is a special occasion maybe?
  • No plans to make everyone's drops a regular thing, we still want to use them for seasonal and special events only. It just so happened that they layered up by the end of 2022 and it was a bit more than we are all used too
  • Thanks for a fun little idea, Falcore: https://discord.com/channels/550315686636027908/983296442145845378/1061602895293849650
I'll bring it up with Jon when we chat tomorrow, see if we can play around with that without making it too annoying.
  • Can confirm that you'll see good old Ebon Flintlock making a return as a Drop soon this year , but hush-hush and no leaks plz or I'll replace it with Twilight Hunter Hull or something, ok?
 (edited)
2
2
1
1
H1gh Voltage
Love to see lots of feedback on Twitch Drops and, like Jo said earlier, it's a great topic to discuss on our next Partner call in a few days. We plan to reveal future plans for the Drops there anyway, so I don't want to spill it all out now, but please accept this long text in response: Before Christmas break we have reviewed tons of data on how all kinds of Drops affected both partnered and non-partnered channels, our own channel and SoT category as a whole and had some discussions with our Cosmetics team. The main takeaways are:
  • Twitch Drops are doing great things for everybody (including our Social Media and website traffic), so no point in making them less frequent.
  • It's not just Drops and giveaways that drive good viewership up. Naturally, in-game content updates (Seasons, Adventures, major patches) are more significant in creating the spikes and general interest around watching and playing SoT. So if we all want SoT to perform great on Twitch, the state of the game and it's content are super important.
  • You'd be surprised, but despite all these "we want more unique drops" sentiments shouted both at you as Partners and at us at Rare... there is actually very little difference in how many players claim the Drops.
Whether it's a recolour or something unique - players claim about the same amount of items every time, with the exception of sets that were used a lot in the past and just lost their appeal over time. Creating whole new exclusive sets is simply not worth it when good quality recolours with slight geometry tweaks are performing just as well at the moment. We'll keep monitoring though to see if things change.
  • Exclusive items tied to specific Twitch live events (like the Orb) create a very intriguing area to explore for our Creator Engagement team. Hopefully we can do something about it this year. And addressing a few questions/bits of feedback from above:
  • No OG Morningstar as it is a Tall Tale set
  • We don't intend to re-use Partner Drops as everyone's Drops soon after the original release. At least until they get very old and there is a special occasion maybe?
  • No plans to make everyone's drops a regular thing, we still want to use them for seasonal and special events only. It just so happened that they layered up by the end of 2022 and it was a bit more than we are all used too
  • Thanks for a fun little idea, Falcore: https://discord.com/channels/550315686636027908/983296442145845378/1061602895293849650
I'll bring it up with Jon when we chat tomorrow, see if we can play around with that without making it too annoying.
  • Can confirm that you'll see good old Ebon Flintlock making a return as a Drop soon this year , but hush-hush and no leaks plz or I'll replace it with Twilight Hunter Hull or something, ok?
 (edited)

Captain Falcore 24/01/2023 11:03 AM
A little feedback about the drops. Lurkers are always a thing, and I always appreciate a lurker. But i'm noticing with the increase of almost bi-weekly drops that lurkers are becoming more and more present throughout all channels. Signifcantly less chatting going on in every single streamers chat when drops are active. The feedback i'm getting from people is that drops are so regular now, and "Gotta catch them all!" that more people are inclined to AFK for the drops. Now although that's doing great things for the games coverage, it's not really helping out the streamer much, especially those with 100-200 views. You can't draw people in and keep them there if they're not behind the keyboard! (edited)
11:08 AM
Also, another bit of feedback. As a whole, especially in my chat, people hate the weekend drops. As someone so eloquently put it "I watch streamers to entertain me at work, I want to actually PLAY the game at the weekend, not watch someone"
11:11 AM
You could see this in numbers back when it was a specific drop a day. Fridays was always booming with around 50-60k people watching Sea of Thieves. Saturday and Sunday was always around 30k. And then monday was BOOM back up to 50-60k
Captain Falcore 24/01/2023 11:33 AM
It's also creating the mentality of "No drops? No point in watching then". Seeing about a 20% drop in views from regular category views when drops are not on.
H1gh Voltage 24/01/2023 12:01 PM
Appreciate the feedback! I don't think that drops really create the "No drops? No point in watching" mentality. It just highlights other issues like "how watchable and fun a particular creator or the game is?" at the current state. There may be other releases, seasonal dips, overall change in sentiment that leads to "I'm not interested, just here for free stuff" mentality. But the point about the weekends is an interesting one to consider. I've already tried to plan the drops around weeks where there are no major content updates this year. We may try to shift the dates on few of them and see if, for example, Wed-Fri works better?

I'd love to try a wed-fri and see how it goes

Are we not bored of this same conversation every month when rare have said they have analytics to back up their reasoning for drops frequency? The reason sot is down generally in the categories is due to a multitude of factors. The cheater problem (which seems to have massively subsided tbf) New games (sons of the forest took nearly a million viewers from other categories) And that's just one game Not to mention I've streamed this game since launch and Jan/Feb numbers are always generally lower, as they are always at the mid point between updates There are a plethora of games on twitch that see creators of all sizes grow with no drops whatsoever and there are games that see drops all the time and no one grows. Twitch is a huge luck of the draw thing and games like DayZ are a great example here, never really more than 5k watching whenever I look after the games been out for nearly a decade yet it still sees people grow. Streamers should have something to aspire to, whether it's partner, sot partner, whatever it may be and to be Frank, a partner program should have perks, it should be a symbiotic relationship that benefits everyone within the program. Now. Having everyone drops alongside partner drops sure I'm for that idea. But again, rare have already stated when they will do this and how. Tl/dr we don't have the analytics. Rare do, I look forward to reposting this public service announcement when we have the same discussion next month (edited)
6
7:03 AM
BehavingBeardly 25/02/2023 8:54 AM
Ayyyy Beard saved me having to write the same rant.... again
9:00 AM
I know no ones saying stop partner drops but just to reaffirm why partner exclusives are SO important (along side everyone drops) is its (whether you like it or not) one of the key features that makes people STRIVE to be in the program... it makes creators work SO MUCH harder on improving their content and hitting those goals which has much more long term benefits for the category and game awareness. If there was NO partner perks and everyone got them anyway then no one would care and put no where NEAR as much effort in... (edited)
BehavingBeardly 25/02/2023 9:16 AM
My favourite saying... its a buisness not a charity....
Captain Falcore 25/02/2023 9:22 AM

BehavingBeardly 25/02/2023 9:23 AM

Captain Falcore 25/02/2023 9:23 AM

BehavingBeardly 25/02/2023 9:24 AM

Captain Falcore 25/02/2023 9:25 AM

H1gh Voltage 25/02/2023 10:43 AM
Ok, here are my two (hundred) cents on the topic from a perspective of an actual person scheduling and setting Drops up: Fun fact - good recolours and items with completely original designs get claimed by players in about the same amounts. There is simply no evidence to support the need to add more work and pressure on our current cosmetics team to make them constantly come up with something new just for the Drops alone. At least not in the quantities required to support whole year worth of Drops both for Partners and everyone else. Will we ever be in a position when this changes and we have the opportunity? Maybe, but not in the nearest future. Growth of the category depends way more on combination of how well the game is doing by itself and what content is being created, also seasonal stuff and other competing releases. Drops are there to help hype up specific events and releases and also to support the game and it's creators during quieter times. But they are not driving the category forward much on their own. When it comes to everyone's drops... they are there and done very regularly. We've had Season 8 Launch drops, huge clothing set on Winter Holidays, couple of rare cool items "from the vault" reserved for each Community Days and please don't forget we've got S9 and Anniversary incoming. So everyone's drops are practically a thing almost every month now. To the point where some Partners here even claimed it was too much free goodness to handle. Setting up drops for everyone on top of Partner exclusive stuff doesn't make a lot of sense in SoT case. Firstly, why would some of your viewers leave you for a much smaller random streamer if they can claim all of the freebies from your channel anyway? I may be wrong, but you may have likely referred to what Avalanche Studios did with Hogwarts Legacy running two types of drops at the same time. However, their context is very different and it's a single-player AAA packaged, shipped and forgotten after a patch or two type of game. It made sense to do everyone's drops with more regular looking cosmetics and then reserve 2 slightly more exciting for their own channel only, because they only streamed a few times for a an hour or two and likely won't do anything with their channel until they announce a big DLC or Hogwarts Legacy 2. If you were thinking more like "some items to be claimed from non-Partners" and "few others to be claimed from Partners only", that is simply impossible to set up from dev.twitch side. I can either choose everyone or an allowed list, no option for exceptions. (edited)
1
1
Captain Falcore 25/02/2023 10:50 AM
The Serpents Lie sails are a good indicator as to people just want stuff because its stuff Almost every bit of feedback I get for them is "THEY SUCK!!" etc. But it's strange how EVERYONE still wants them! Drops variety is exactly the same. And I keep going back to the Twilight Hunter Flag. It was just a dirty green flag, that's ALL it was. Yet, when it was active as the drop, it had the BIGGEST numbers in the category!
Mother0fChickens 25/02/2023 10:52 AM
I'm absolutely not complaining... as one of the smaller partners I freaking love drops! I was just explaining how Hunt run theirs, it wasn't a moan about the way we do ours

Ok, here are my two (hundred) cents on the topic from a perspective of an actual person scheduling and setting Drops up: Fun fact - good recolours and items with completely original designs get claimed by players in about the same amounts. There is simply no evidence to support the need to add more work and pressure on our current cosmetics team to make them constantly come up with something new just for the Drops alone. At least not in the quantities required to support whole year worth of Drops both for Partners and everyone else. Will we ever be in a position when this changes and we have the opportunity? Maybe, but not in the nearest future. Growth of the category depends way more on combination of how well the game is doing by itself and what content is being created, also seasonal stuff and other competing releases. Drops are there to help hype up specific events and releases and also to support the game and it's creators during quieter times. But they are not driving the category forward much on their own. When it comes to everyone's drops... they are there and done very regularly. We've had Season 8 Launch drops, huge clothing set on Winter Holidays, couple of rare cool items "from the vault" reserved for each Community Days and please don't forget we've got S9 and Anniversary incoming. So everyone's drops are practically a thing almost every month now. To the point where some Partners here even claimed it was too much free goodness to handle. Setting up drops for everyone on top of Partner exclusive stuff doesn't make a lot of sense in SoT case. Firstly, why would some of your viewers leave you for a much smaller random streamer if they can claim all of the freebies from your channel anyway? I may be wrong, but you may have likely referred to what Avalanche Studios did with Hogwarts Legacy running two types of drops at the same time. However, their context is very different and it's a single-player AAA packaged, shipped and forgotten after a patch or two type of game. It made sense to do everyone's drops with more regular looking cosmetics and then reserve 2 slightly more exciting for their own channel only, because they only streamed a few times for a an hour or two and likely won't do anything with their channel until they announce a big DLC or Hogwarts Legacy 2. If you were thinking more like "some items to be claimed from non-Partners" and "few others to be claimed from Partners only", that is simply impossible to set up from dev.twitch side. I can either choose everyone or an allowed list, no option for exceptions. (edited)

PhuzzyBond 26/02/2023 7:01 AM
Yeah drops are mainly related to the update hype, and what other games are trending, during Captaincy Drops, season 7, I peaked at almost 6K viewers. Obviously due to the update itself being hype, my usual is 1.5-3K during Drops like current one.
7:04 AM
Also I can confirm, during Everyone drops I always get more viewers than partner only drops and i use that to raid a small streamer after hopefully giving them that "tick" to finally bring their streams to light, some of them now average at 40 viewers instead of the 10-15 they used to get since these raids . Always awesome during everyone drops to Gather the viewers and catapult that to a life changing moment for a small streamer

thanks for the drops seriously. PoisonHearrt 27/02/2023 7:16 PM
TY for putting a lot of the drops close to the end of the month. It really comes in handy & trying to hang in there in hopes for a check mark has been very stressful. I give my all & that's all I can do but doing it while others can get SoT drops usually brings around memories of Rare games through the decades & what the company has always meant to me. I will continue to work my ass off even if it has to be way less hours a month than I would prefer but all are still loving the fact that you can get all drops 1 per hour & my Community knows they can count on me to grind as much in SoT as possible, working on goals in game along w/ streaming goals in general. I had over 120k+ hours watched before becoming a SoT Partner & proud to say that next month I will be hitting my 1st Kind of a Big Deal milestone on Twitch & I wouldn't have gotten to that point w/o the streaming changes that have happened of being a proud member of this program. TY Rare for all you do & I will always try to give all the energy I can to show my Community how much SoT means to me. ❤️
2
1
LetsDrewThis 1/03/2023 3:14 PM
For those succeeding during drops: I'm happy for you. Truly. I'm glad things are working well for you. For those who are struggling, even with drops: I feel for you. I'm there with you. But I know we'll get through it somehow. For those who need to hear it: Have a bit of empathy. Put yourself in someone else's shoes. If you've been in those shoes, remember what it was like. We all worked hard to be here and still work hard to maintain what we have. There is a great rift growing between partners lately. It's obvious. Try to all get back on the same page again.

PoisonHearrt 2/03/2023 7:57 PM
and I thank you so much for keeping us in mind. All that have, I save a pic to always keep things in mind & try to always tweet a TY when the moments happen. You helped it pop for being to apply for Partnership in October since my average was at 67. Trying my best & although so many feel I will get there w/ the checkmark it's always some type of set back like almost hitting it or inconsistent from viewership but I just keep doing what I do & now have to have 2 surgeries so not sure how bad that will effect things in the long run. I don't have time to edit things for other media since I found out my entire house is wired wrong to heavy powered things are on 2nd line & lil things are on main line so that will all have to be corrected leaving tons of drywall to also be repaired etc. I am thankful to all for everything & I thank you for being thought of. I struggle at times to think I am equal tbh but even if I am in my corner with a few pals & my mods to sail with, I am still as happy as can be to be a SoT Partner telling my stories & accomplishing all I do in this incredible pirate game from an amazing company I have loved for decades. Keep letting those waves flow & enjoy the adventures ❤️ ps. none of this was said as a pitty party of any level. I am a tough cookie & have always gone a tougher route to get anywhere. I'm just being honest before I go quiet again until I feel it is a strong enough subject to chime in on my pov. (edited)

H1gh Voltage 22/05/2023 12:29 PM
Everyone drops follow wherever the Launch date for S10 is, so yeah. If it stays the same, the drops will also have to happen the following weekend. Everyone drops follow wherever the Launch date for S10 is, so yeah. If it stays the same, the drops will also have to happen the following weekend. PhuzzyBond 9/06/2023 3:14 PM
Twitch Drops Feedback: In the past, we were fortunate to receive remarkable Twitch drops that consistently amazed viewers. However, the current drops feel lackluster, often mere recolors. Take, for example, the Eastern Winds Sapphire Set, Eastern Winds Ruby Set, and others. While we genuinely appreciate the drops, they give the impression that "let's quickly recolor something and include it." Even if this is not the case, it is the prevailing perception among the majority. The numbers across the board support this observation, which may also be influenced by the state of the game at any given time. Considering Sea of Thieves is a cosmetic-based game, I propose exploring alternative approaches to drop solutions. For instance, consider recoloring Ship Crests, Voyage Tables, and Chairs. This simple change alone could significantly increase viewership across the board. Additionally, we could follow the example set by Rust. Basic ship cosmetics and sets could be available to all Sea of Thieves partners, while Captaincy cosmetics could be divided among different groups of streamers. This way, Larger and smaller partners would receive an equal share of viewership, providing smaller partners a chance to keep up with the numbers and grow further.
BehavingBeardly 9/06/2023 3:17 PM
recolour of halo set... Red Partner Drops, Blue Everyone Drops.... EZ W
3:17 PM
🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 9/06/2023 3:53 PM
We are unlikely to offer captiancy items as drops - these are gated items that require a captained ships. Clothing, instruments and equipment are something everyone can use. I believe Anna touched on this before, but the drops should be moving towards being seperated more with say eastern winds followed by another set then back to eastern winds then another set, throw in a new recolour etc etc. It's just with what we had made and ready, this is how the cards have fallen for now. Truly unique items will not go to drops, or at least it's very unlikely - these items are made for in game shops and the emporium. Appreciate the feedback re: creator crew though as that's a programme I am currently (slowly) attacking and trying to give some love to but it's easier said than done when people can't behave for more than 48 hours

Something I’ve been wondering about lately is with the recent twitch policies changes, are there any plans to have drops on other platforms in the future? Or will they only ever be on twitch?
1
Captain Falcore 14/06/2023 8:59 PM
Its important to emphasise that the Twitch changes are not about advertisments as a whole. But rather "Burnt in" advertisments. Their language - as they put up for defence, but quite frankly is backtracking - made it look like people could not use their own ads, or ad videos etc (because that's literally what they said). What they MEANT however, was burnt in ads from external sources. Such as a 3rd party advertisment party selling ads to streamer to burn into their stream. Putting up your own ad as a video, or displaying a banner ad from your own OBS source is fine. Therefore, Twitch Drops will be unaffected.
8:59 PM
TLDR. Twitch are stupid.
9:02 PM
Been doing this rodeo 13 years now, and there is always some controversy every 6 months or so which, most of the time, is people reading a policy wrong and taking it as "The Death of <insert social platform>. But on this occasion, Twitch LITERALLY stated people cannot run their own ads on their streams. And then later said "Oh no, we didn't mean it like that". It's like someone saying "By the way, you can rob that bank if you want to, totally legal now!" and then later saying "Oh when we said that, we meant, don't rob that bank, its illegal"
PhuzzyBond 15/06/2023 6:33 AM
Twitch doing this only makes competitions stronger, and competition is always healthy, since its always us that win Remember when the Mixer hype happened? twitch added over 10 new amazing features in less than a month, because they were worried. Twitch Drop Notification Twitch Taiwan are hosting a SoT tournament! The event will be running from 28th June to 9th July 2023 with livestreaming, hourly Twitch Drops and Steam codes giveaway during the weekends. Skirmish and Qualifiers - June 28th, July 1st and 2nd Rising Morningstar Battle Hat Rising Morningstar Belt Rising Morningstar Boots Rising Morningstar Crew Gloves Rising Morningstar Crew Jacket Rising Morningstar Hook Finals - July 7th - July 9th Rising Morningstar Trousers Rising Morningstar Captain Gloves Rising Morningstar Dress Rising Morningstar Dress Jacket Rising Morningstar Dress Hat Rising Morningstar Pegleg These drops are not a Sea of Thieves event. Please refer to twitch for further information. This notice is only to inform everyone that there is this a drops event. As for which stations can get twitch drops, please go to https://www.twitch.tv/drops/campaigns
Twitch
Twitch is the world's leading video platform and community for gamers.

BehavingBeardly 9/08/2023 6:42 PM
I'm sure you've seen it/aware but just in case @H1gh Voltage. Twitch are changing Drops infrastructure for Drops https://discuss.dev.twitch.tv/t/improving-the-flexibility-of-twitch-drops/46818
Improving the Flexibility of Twitch Drops
Twitch Drops is a self-service product that provides game developers and publishers the ability to grant in-game rewards to the Twitch community for watching streamers playing their game. It brings the community closer to the game, ultimately helping to acquire new players and re-engage an existing player base. As we plan the next chapter, Twitc...

Captain Falcore 21/08/2023 9:38 PM
One of the biggest bits of feedback I got from this drops for everyone was "Why don't we get the rest of the Mutinious Fist ship sett!?" Can I suggest the next "Drops for everyone" does exactly that! Would be very nicely received. Concerning drops as a whole though whether its everyone or just Partner Drops. This is a list of already released drops that people keep asking about in every single drop.
  • Ancestral Set
  • Gears of War set
  • Original and newer Halo set (shipset)
  • Frozen Horizon
Another thing people are always asking for is Gilded Voyages or something similar to that for Twitch Drops. As well as a Gilded Voyage quest that focuses on Humble/Generous Gifts. But that's also just a general bit of feedback for the game. Really nice shipset up for grabs that most of us only got due to the events that surrounded them at the time. Pretty hard to get now.

MonkyJV 🇫🇷 17/11/2023 12:56 PM

MonkyJV 🇫🇷
For your information, all 4 drops activate at the same time, but you need to watch a live stream for 4 hours to unlock them. However, you don't need to validate them every hour to get them. Is this normal?
H1gh Voltage 17/11/2023 2:13 PM
I'll copy my reply to AAs here: Basically, since October Twitch portal update there is no option to set up dependency between claiming drops anymore, so in order to replicate the way they always used to work we have to set up timers like this. In the end, It's same 4 hours to unlock all drops for the viewers in the order intended. But progress now looks different.

WEEK LONG DROPS Hate to offer this feedback as schedule wise, doing drops for a week from a Tuesday works SO WELL for me but I feel it damages the 'excitement' for drops. 7:54 AM
8:01 AM
But concerning recent drops, the lack of hype really is down to what's on offer. We've gone from "I need that!" to "I'll have that, i guess". A plain example of this, was, i think... During the famine of Season 9 when the category was lucky to hit 3k during normal days. We had Morningstar Items. Infact if memory serves me right, there was one piece that was totally new. Category then, when nothing special was happening, no event, no hype generated from any other source, saw the category shoot up to 70-75k. Last time we saw numbers close to that was when the Obsidian Rug was up for grabs. The drops are just dull, saturated, and boring. We shouldn't be offering 2nd or even 3rd variations of the same cosmetic set. Those should just be in the game to help push the incentive.
8:02 AM
I wouldn't care if there were 17 recolours of the Eastern Winds set. As long as we kept the ball rolling on moved onto the next ASAP.
8:05 AM
Also, I would love to see some new focus for drops. Rather than just "heres a glove". A good example is something along the lines of the Grogmany Tankard, that updates the more times you take part. Imagine a sword for example that gets encrusted with a new jewel everytime you watch a Drop session.
8:05 AM
Call it the Infinity Sword for the meme's. And stick that baby on every Community Week, activate everyone drops, and stick it on the for the 4th hour slot. (edited)
8:08 AM
Or add a quest for a drop, I dunno... Ashen Gilded Athena or something. Or maybe even a Special Fort of the Damned, that pops into your "Special Voyages" tab on the quest table.
Captain Falcore 28/08/2024 8:20 AM

NateaCuiki 28/08/2024 4:53 PM
I think the main problem is that Drops have not been interesting for many people for too long, and they have stopped being enthusiastic about them. That's why I think it's better to change the style of drops a bit and not give long sets. If someone doesn't like the style of a set, they have to wait 6 or 7 months for it to end to get something different and pray that the next set will be to their liking. It would be much better if the Drops were more varied items and caused more surprise when they are announced. The idea of ​​creating purple pets could be a very interesting thing. Put a purple pet in the 4th final slot of the drops, and then in the Emporium inject more clothes and accessories for that type. And in the following Drops another type of purple pet with more accessories of its type to buy. It would be a good strategy, and people would love that Drop, even if the first 3 items didn't interest them that much.
Captain Falcore 28/08/2024 5:08 PM
Yeah 100% agree, put the filler fluff in the first 3 hours. And something really special in the 4th hour.

Captain Falcore 29/08/2024 12:53 PM
Twitch is honestly a secret weapon. Every single time this game has done extrenely well (outside of hype updates) has been becuase of Twitch. When a popular streamer who gets 20k per stream plays, the category gets a massive boost for several days, and more players swarm in. Or if a drop campaign is very hype, pushing the category to the top ten for example. The category again gets a massive boost, and more players swarm in. Isolating this godly power by adding totally unmissable drops is one of the best marketting deals you guys can do.... Like... Stick a Pirate Emporium set on for Twitch Drops, one of the old ones... MonkyJV 🇫🇷 8/10/2024 4:58 PM
In the latest Sea of Thieves News, I made a video presenting the new features of Season 14 for the French Community. I've had a lot of negative feedback on Twitch Drops. I agree with @Captain Falcore, the community deserves better Twitch Drops. Twitch Drops are starting to get a very bad image at the moment. I'm sure you can come up with better ideas for Twitch Drops, so don't hesitate to listen to Partners' advice. I'm going to give my opinion now: I find the Twitch Drops offered at the moment very lazy. It's either Reskin or... reskin on basic skins. I think Twitch Drops should be much more exclusive and elegant. I hope that the quality of the drops will improve, because a lot of players are fed up with the current Twitch Drops, which don't appeal at all.
MonkyJV 🇫🇷
In the latest Sea of Thieves News, I made a video presenting the new features of Season 14 for the French Community. I've had a lot of negative feedback on Twitch Drops. I agree with @Captain Falcore, the community deserves better Twitch Drops. Twitch Drops are starting to get a very bad image at the moment. I'm sure you can come up with better ideas for Twitch Drops, so don't hesitate to listen to Partners' advice. I'm going to give my opinion now: I find the Twitch Drops offered at the moment very lazy. It's either Reskin or... reskin on basic skins. NateaCuiki 8/10/2024 5:16 PM
I agree. I've also commented here about the need for Drops to be a better marketing strategy. The problem isn't just that Drops are reskins, it's that they could be reskins of VERY nice sets. For example, a Gold Leaf set reskin, among many other new designs that, with the appropriate colors, people would love. The idea of ​​making Drops of purple pets, and then incorporating more clothes into the Emporium is also a very profitable strategy. Anyway, we've reached a point where it seems that Drops are the leftovers of the most boring cosmetics that were in the drawer, and they need to be gotten rid of. But it's a mistake to do that, because Drops are a very interesting way of promoting the game that can't be left to die like this.

MonkyJV 🇫🇷 24/10/2024 6:28 AM
Hello everyone, I haven't had time to do this with the stream and work, but I wanted to give you my feedback on the latest drops. I had MUCH less people on these drops than on all the other drops. The drops are less interesting for the spectators. There are several reasons for this, but that's just my point of view:
  • Sea of Thieves has a very bad reputation because of the numerous bugs, cheats, exploits and harassment. I'm aware that it's very hard to develop and maintain a game, but I believe that the game has never been in such poor health... And that really hurts me, knowing how much work you put in!
  • Because of its bad reputation, players, viewers, streamers and even creators are playing the game less or thinking of abandoning it altogether.
  • The drops aren't attractive at all. The quest was an excellent idea, well done! But it was still very short! I think you wanted to make the quest accessible to everyone, but it's a shame to have an exclusive quest that provides so little game time and almost no rewards.
  • Sorry to say it, but Season 14 was a failure. The community wasn't really looking forward to Season 14 in the first place (which wasn't the case for me), so delivering a Season 14 with half the content didn't please them at all. Even though I know it was the best decision to remove the traps and suspend themselves because they were causing problems in-game, it was very badly received. Even worse, when you removed the crouching, but again it was necessary.
It's not all bad, I did get a bit of a turnout, but for a Season launch, in terms of my audience, it was very worrying! The other Seasons have done much better and the drops for this Season launch didn't help at all.
2oldRudi 24/10/2024 8:45 AM

SoT numbres are dropping, that's a fact, and drops are not working as good as before. But there's clearly a potential into making more unique stuff to bring more people during these periods. Weapons work well, shipsets work well, obsidian anything works well.

Captain Falcore 10/11/2024 11:56 AM
Twitch Drops are a marketing tool, not intended to help individual content creators generate more views or recognition. Rather, they are designed to promote a game or activity, increasing overall trend and awareness, which can then lead to more sales and collaborations. The recent Twitch Drops offerings, however, have been lackluster. The issue is not with the items themselves, but rather the way they are rolled out. Providing multiple, fragmented sets instead of allowing users to complete a single set has created a negative narrative around Twitch Drops. It's been two years, and users still haven't even finished one full Eastern Winds set, which is actually a record in terms of Twitch Drops. Maintaining a consistent set, such as the Ruby Eastern Winds, would have allowed viewers to easily follow along as new drops occurred. This approach has been tested and proven successful, as the data clearly demonstrates. While I understand the team is stretched thin and recolors are the only option for Twitch Drops, I believe offering a single unique item from the Pirate Emporium, such as a sword skin, sail skin, or trinket, would be more effective. This approach, commonly used in other Twitch Drops programs, would generate more revenue and drive greater interest in the full set of items. Providing a desirable, limited-time item as part of a Twitch Drop promotion could significantly boost both game sales and engagement. This process of offering Twitch drops has been widely adopted and proven effective in many gaming categories. Nowadays, Twitch Drops no longer feel like genuine incentives, but rather like hastily-added afterthoughts. As a consumer, I mean this constructively - the pacing of their rollout and the quality of the items themselves give an impression of laziness and lack of care. The Drops have lost the sense of reward and excitement they once had. A recent example demonstrates the issue - an almost identical shovel was released through two separate Twitch Drops. How can such redundant content be considered a good practice on paper? Twitch Drops are a powerful marketing tool that could generate significant revenue for the game, potentially even exceeding that of the Pirate Emporium. This strategy deserves serious consideration and a more robust implementation than it currently has. The data shows the current plan is simply not working, and this has nothing to do with the game's age, patch popularity, or other external factors. For example, an Obsidian Crest promotion was able to drive viewership to nearly 90,000, even when the category was typically only seeing around 4,000 views at peak times. This suggests the right plan could help the game consistently achieve those high viewership numbers. Rare should convene a meeting to develop a new, more effective plan of action for leveraging Twitch Drops to the game's advantage. Sorry for wall of text (edited)
5
4
12:01 PM
The community was significantly more excited about the quest in the last Twitch Drops than the previous Twitch Drops offerings. While incorporating more engaging quests into future Twitch Drops could be beneficial, it's not a simple solution. The key point is that players were more enthusiastic about the prospect of searching for barrels than receiving a mere shovel as a reward. (edited)
Captain Falcore 15/11/2024 12:27 PM
I understand that drops are typically not released on weekends, but rolling out the Partner Drops over this weekend was a poor choice given the community's low morale. Even at the start of this campaign, it is clearly struggling. While the team's efforts to lift spirits are commendable, this is not the optimal time to be showcasing the game. Midweek drops followed by the Everyone Drops rollout would have generated much more excitement, especially with the potential for an early next week update. Scheduling the drops to end on the same day as the proposed update release feels like adding insult to injury.
2

I've never had so few people on my livestream during a Twitch Drops campaign... But I'm not surprised, given the Twitch Drops... And you guys?
Captain Falcore 25/01/2025 11:01 AM
The viewership for the category has been steadily declining for a long time. While the lackluster Twitch Drops are part of the problem, they are not the root cause. This is now a very small category, Even with somewhat hyped Twitch Drops, the category peaks at around 15,000 viewers, spread across 40-50 livestreams. With massive drop offs in view counts. Some peaking to 1-3k viewership, and then a massive drop off with "next in line" at 300/200 views. It's a very unhealthy category. The main issues are a combination of updates that missed the mark, poor communication, and the game simply being quite old at this point. Better Twitch Drops could provide a small boost, but the core problem is the game's current state.
1
Puff 25/01/2025 11:52 AM
Drops in my experience, as a smaller creator in the category, is always a hit or miss. Surprisingly it's been a really good run for me this time. Probably because I've been doing things differently in terms of content, time that I start stream and more variety in people I play with. "Everyone" drops tend to be more of a gamble by nature.

Captain Falcore 6/05/2025 12:11 PM
Concerning #announcements . Excellent work guys! This will increase hype and engagment like never before, glad you're finally putting focus into the drops. Just a small piece of feedback: if you're planning to introduce Twitch "legacy cosmetics" and release them in a drip-feed manner, it would be immensely helpful (and slightly less maddening) to showcase the full set in its entirety upfront. That way, players can at least mentally prepare for the inevitable hype attrition. Then, simply clarify which item will be released first. Transparency prevents torches and pitchforks later.
4

Also, with a massive pool such as this. It should be a week-long drop campaign.

Captain Falcore
Concerning #announcements . Excellent work guys! This will increase hype and engagment like never before, glad you're finally putting focus into the drops. Just a small piece of feedback: if you're planning to introduce Twitch "legacy cosmetics" and release them in a drip-feed manner, it would be immensely helpful (and slightly less maddening) to showcase the full set in its entirety upfront. H1gh Voltage 6/05/2025 12:44 PM
I think we're going to switch things up with legacy Drops too. Last thing we want is to commit to, say, a Twilight Hunter set (for example) and then run it until we die since it's just one item per month. So it is meant to be randomised, sometimes fitting the theme of the whole Drops bundle or Season, sometimes not. (edited)

Savage Alien [Wiki] 6/05/2025 4:13 PM
The Unique Cosmetics idea is an interesting one. I'd be in favour of the "mini collection" idea. We get some fun standalones each Season [FRORB!] which people love and are really solid standalones that don't need full sets - but on the flip side people also love when items match together, and I certainly see comments wishing for certain existing standalones to be expanded. Drops have certainly been a victim to Eastern Winds fatigue having gone through Sapphire and Ruby since 2022. The recent change in item have piqued interest again - and I think these changes will too!

Reapers Rise and we get a Spring Blossum pistol? C'mon guys.

Captain Falcore 14/05/2025 5:51 PM
I was genuinely excited about this new approach to drops, but the execution feels underwhelming and doesn't instill much confidence. It's hard to see this having a meaningful impact on the growth of the category. Additionally, scheduling drops on launch day when server issues are almost guaranteed continues to be problematic. It creates a poor first impression for new players and reflects poorly on the game's overall experience. We continue to provide valuable feedback on this issue, and while there's acknowledgment that it's being heard, we’ve yet to see any meaningful changes implemented.
Savage Alien [Wiki] 14/05/2025 5:57 PM

It's still a nice way to gather and sample random premium items, though I guess.
Captain Falcore 14/05/2025 5:59 PM

OffenddCamel 21/06/2025 2:20 PM
Just throwing an idea out there, what if Partner Drops ran in a 4 day cycle split into two phases? • Day 1: Drops A, B, C, D • Day 2: Drops E, F, G, H • Day 3: Back to A, B, C, D • Day 4: Back to E, F, G, H That way, viewers get variety and if someone misses a day, they still have a second shot to grab the same rewards. Just some food for thought!

Captain Falcore 28/08/2025 8:27 PM
Honestly, all I’m asking is that you truly listen to the feedback coming from your community about Twitch Drops , and stop relying solely on internal data to gauge how things are going. Although I doubt this is the case, as Drops have been in a 15% decline each and every time in 3 years, and that has nothing to do with the age of the game. Please stop pretending everything is fine and pass this feedback along to the people who actually make these decisions. Take the Hunter’s Call update, for example. That Hunter’s Call hawk should have absolutely been one of the drops , a perfect 6-hour reward that players could genuinely get excited about. Pair that with something like a 50% sale on pet outfits in the Emporium, and now you’ve got a campaign that drives both engagement and sales. I mean, Hunters Call Drops and not ONE fishing rod? I mean c'moooonnnn guys! What are you doing over there? Instead, we’re getting recycled or underwhelming items, things that were either never in high demand, or that feel like leftovers from the cutting room floor. "Twilight Hunter"? "Eastern Winds"? Really? It’s hard to get hyped when the rewards feel more like clearance-bin filler than a meaningful part of the event. These kinds of missteps are frustrating because the potential is right there. You have one of the strongest community engagement tools in the industry with Twitch, and yet it feels like it’s being treated as an afterthought. Yes, the new drops system is slightly better than previous versions , but only just. We’re still choosing between a rotten apple and a rotten potato. Look at what other games are doing with Twitch campaigns, not to copy them, but to understand how impactful these systems can be when done right. I get that the people reading this might not be the ones deciding what gets included in the drops, but if you can, please pass this along to the relevant team. Because right now, the system feels disappointing. And with just a bit more intention and creativity, it could be something the community actually celebrates. I love you guys, but honestly. Sort out your drop campaigns, you're sitting on gold right here and throwing it away each and every time! C'mon! (edited)
3

Captain Falcore 31/08/2025 2:35 PM
Given the recurring issues during Community Weekend, such as confusion around drops, miscommunication both on social media and in here, and problems with people either not receiving items or getting the wrong ones. Would it not make more sense to shift from a “Community Weekend” to a “Community Week” format instead? This isn't meant as criticism or blame, just an observation based on patterns we've seen almost every time. A longer timeframe with better weekday coverage might allow for smoother execution, clearer communication, and quicker support if things go sideways. Just something to consider for the future!
2
🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 31/08/2025 3:29 PM
We cant do a Community Week - the toll it takes on the CM team means it would interfere with our job for an entire week - we do these weekends as an extra on top of our day to day and running these, and work the entire weekend to pull it off - PuPs, hats, and all the other stuff simply isnt possible during a working week. Not to mention a week of PuP maps isnt feasible as each on is indivdually thought up with accompanying maps etc. Also a lot of stuff is reactive meaning we are sitting watching dashboards etc all weekend, as well as the social postings and announcements. These are run as bonuses by the CM team. We've taken note of the issues and it's a learning for us to discuss for next time given the change in format for a few things this time round. (edited)
3
3:33 PM
P.S. Not to say we wont ever do it - but doing it consistently is a lot. Would love to manage it as a treat some time, maybe.

ACCREATOR 13/09/2025 8:11 AM

jjgaymer☠ 17/09/2025 3:35 PM
@Partners @Affiliate Anna and I have been having a think and were looking at this approach based on feedback in the Discord. It is similar to that of Hunt Showdown and hopefully satisfies as mush as possible the asks for a balanced approach. I want to share this approach with you all an get your thoughts on it. If you have comments please let me know but if you could also give me a thumbs up and down vote to let me gauge clearly you thoughts on it all. Here are are some of the reasons why we came to this. 10am UTC starts have to stay. While it would be great to change at midnight this isn't within the studios working hours so if there is an issue we can't deal with it. 10am UTC is our quietest time (during working hours) for viewers anyway so makes sense to switch then. Adding in the split in days already adds a layer of complexity for viewers. So we want to avoid splitting the items in the lineup as well, we feel this would cause way too much confusion and would be very hard to communicate. We see the majority of our claims happen in the period from Friday to Sunday so this still gives a good exclusive window for Partners. While opening it up to the rest of the directory later in the week helps keep momentum and offers smaller creators a chance to draw in those who might have missed it. Also worth noting that Twitch do not let us do two sets of concurrent drops. This means we can't have to campaigns that run at the same time or overlap. Just for clarity this would start from the next drops in October if we agree. (edited)
9
3:36 PM
Leator 17/09/2025 3:41 PM
While this seems like a good compromise for partners. I fear this might not go down well with viewers. Watching 6 hours on a weekend? And if they fail to claim, let‘s say the last drop, they have to retry and watching 6 hours, again, during working days? This situation is like being stuck between a rock and a hard place (edited)
