NessieDoes 17/09/2025 4:02 PM
I believe the way forward is either keep drops as they are (sometimes partner sometimes everyone) or make them everyone drops all the time with no annoying two drop campaigns within a singular week I think this idea is pretty BAD (no offense) because non partners will get a worse experience during EVERY single drops campaign. I think it is common knowledge that drops perform best on the first couple days which means the proposed everyone drops idea from a few days ago (https://discord.com/channels/550315686636027908/625611888956276736/1415734458270744710) has turned from giving everyone equal opportunity to greatly impacting smaller creators as now they can never fairly compete during drops (edited)
4:08 PM
the last back to back drops during community weekend saw a big decline in views by day 4, which would become the norm for non partners to jump on (edited)
4:14 PM
4:15 PM
drops starting august 30th and september 5th
4:16 PM
greater than 50% decline on day 4 (which is what the non partners would get a share of) (edited)
4:19 PM
Sorry to be so negative as I know you are all working hard on this! I just want to jump in as early as possible to say I think this is straying as far away from lifting up all creators (which I assume was the intention of the proposed change to begin with)
World of Chap 17/09/2025 4:43 PM
I think it’s a good idea. I still see increased viewership on the extended drops days, and I think it’s important to continue pushing new creators to strive for sot partner with it as an incentive. With this, there won’t be times where non partners go a full week not wanting to stream at all due to not having drops. Back when Drops weren’t a full week, it didn’t feel as bad to not have access to them. I like this approach personally, it feels pretty fair. But only if the campaign doesn’t restart, I could see that being very confusing for viewers
Captain Falcore 17/09/2025 5:14 PM
If we’re going to roll out Partner Drops, the incentive needs to be truly exclusive. That should be the top priority, because right now the system doesn’t feel special or engaging. Offering “second chance” drops just encourages people to think, “I’ll just lurk while I’m at work and get it later.” There needs to be a clear priority order for Twitch Drops. Partner Drops should feature rare, highly sought-after cosmetics, like the Arena items you’ve been putting in the stores. Those are perfect examples of what would make Partner Drops exciting and valuable. Then, past campaign rewards could be cycled back in as “Everyone Drops,” giving players a fair second chance without diminishing the exclusivity of Partner rewards. The current system isn’t working, you’re missing out on a huge opportunity for marketing and community hype. Right now, the drops feel uninspired and don’t drive engagement. A serious shift in how cosmetics are distributed needs to be discussed internally if you want drops to generate real excitement and visibility. Exclusivity in terms of value from the players Perspective should be.. Emporium Items > Twitch Drops > Log in Rewards > Outpost stores. Right now its Pirate Emporium > Log in Iteams > Outpost Stores > Twitch Drops (edited)
2
NessieDoes
Click to see attachment 🖼️
jjgaymer☠ 17/09/2025 5:15 PM
Here is where my head is at, and I really do hear your point. From a marketing view we want to guide viewers to our Partners because you consistently show the game in its best light even when you are brutally honest at times. Drops usually land right after an update when everyone is hungry to see what is new, and having you be the first stop helps the whole community discover that content quickly. I’ve also seen in the Discord discussions that folks really value this as a Partner Perk. Many of you have even taken big risks, like leaving a full time job to pursue this, and making sure this perk supports your livelihood truly matters to us. That said we do not want talented creators who are still building toward Partner to feel left out. We have more in the works to support that journey and Anna will share details soon. Community Weekends will also continue to give everyone a chance to earn Drops, so they are not going away. Our aim is to recognise Partners while still opening doors for the wider creator community, and we’ll continue listening as things evolve.
1
NessieDoes 17/09/2025 5:23 PM
My message was still trying to build on the initial idea of “all drops being everyone drops” so I didn’t really bother with the we want partners to showcase the game line of thought as that would be out the window with the original idea in announcements. Completely understand the marketing view and keeping a partner perk for us (as peeps have been vocal in here about wanting it) If community weekend drops will still be everyone drops for the full 7 days then I think this proposed solution is a great middle ground. It keeps a partner perk for us and gives all streamers a chance at every drop campaign. But if partners get the 3 day head start on community weekends, I don’t think peeps will be happy with missing out on their opportunity to stream drops the day they launch (edited)
1
Captain Falcore
If we’re going to roll out Partner Drops, the incentive needs to be truly exclusive. That should be the top priority, because right now the system doesn’t feel special or engaging. Offering “second chance” drops just encourages people to think, “I’ll just lurk while I’m at work and get it later.” There needs to be a clear priority order for Twitch Drops. Partner Drops should feature rare, highly sought-after cosmetics, like the Arena items you’ve been putting in the stores. Those are perfect examples of what would make Partner Drops exciting and valuable. Then, past campaign rewards could be cycled back in as “Everyone Drops,” giving players a fair second chance without diminishing the exclusivity of Partner rewards. The current system isn’t working, you’re missing out on a huge opportunity for marketing and community hype. Right now, the drops feel uninspired and don’t drive engagement. A serious shift in how cosmetics are distributed needs to be discussed internally if you want drops to generate real excitement and visibility. Exclusivity in terms of value from the players Perspective should be.. Emporium Items > Twitch Drops > Log in Rewards > Outpost stores. Right now its Pirate Emporium > Log in Iteams > Outpost Stores > Twitch Drops (edited)

Not invalid, just separate.

NateaCuiki 17/09/2025 5:56 PM
I think for Community Weekend drops, it would be much better if were always drops for everyone, as a general celebration for the entire community, and if the campaign only lasted the two days of the event, with the Obsidian drops. At the end of the day, the goal to unlock the extra Obsidian Drops is always met. So I think it would be much better if Obsidian Drops were simply the default drops given out during the two days of Community Weekend, boosting the Twitch category for the duration of the event, and that's it. You can also use previous Obsidian drops that people may have missed, as was done this last time with the table. We had the table that had already been given out and the Logbook as something new. And regarding drops for partners and everyone, the format of giving drops to partners for the first 3 days and then unlocking them for everyone starting on Monday could be a good idea, as long as the change doesn't require redoing a new campaign, meaning the progression that viewers have completed would continue when the status changes to everyone. Finally, I understand that Falcore's comment seems like a separate and deeper topic, but I also fully agree with what he mentioned. Cosmetics are being used to be sold at Outposts for gold, which would have been perfect for Partner Drops, and in many cases, cosmetics are being offered for Drops that feel less valuable.

Leator 18/09/2025 5:29 AM
Also... I've heard it from so many people... There are too much Drops this month...
5:31 AM
We're talking 20 days of Drops in the 30 days of September. We could just look at the 4 weeks after last Friday of August, 23 days of drops in a 28 day window.
jjgaymer☠
@Partners @Affiliate Anna and I have been having a think and were looking at this approach based on feedback in the Discord. It is similar to that of Hunt Showdown and hopefully satisfies as mush as possible the asks for a balanced approach. I want to share this approach with you all an get your thoughts on it. If you have comments please let me know but if you could also give me a thumbs up and down vote to let me gauge clearly you thoughts on it all. Here are are some of the reasons why we came to this. 10am UTC starts have to stay. While it would be great to change at midnight this isn't within the studios working hours so if there is an issue we can't deal with it. 10am UTC is our quietest time (during working hours) for viewers anyway so makes sense to switch then. Adding in the split in days already adds a layer of complexity for viewers. So we want to avoid splitting the items in the lineup as well, we feel this would cause way too much confusion and would be very hard to communicate. We see the majority of our claims happen in the period from Friday to Sunday so this still gives a good exclusive window for Partners. While opening it up to the rest of the directory later in the week helps keep momentum and offers smaller creators a chance to draw in those who might have missed it. Also worth noting that Twitch do not let us do two sets of concurrent drops. This means we can't have to campaigns that run at the same time or overlap. Just for clarity this would start from the next drops in October if we agree. (edited)
ACCREATOR 18/09/2025 8:14 AM
I really like this approach of giving a temporary exclusive to partners/AAs and then opening Twitch drops to everyone during the week. I think it's really fair.

jjgaymer☠ 2/10/2025 11:52 AM
Hey @Partners @Affiliate , quick update on the Drops reformat we talked about before. After digging into it, unfortunately the proposed setup is not possible for a couple of reasons. Twitch does not allow us to switch a campaign from partners only to everyone mid run without fully stopping it. Some of you flagged that earlier and you were right. If we did stop it, we would need to create new voyages, gold, and renown for both campaigns which would be a huge extra lift. Because we also cannot overlap two campaigns, the original format just is not workable. The only approach left is the version I posted below. Partners get an exclusive item plus two other rewards, while everyone else gets five rewards, all of which have been available in the past in some way. I used the last Drops set as an example, but moving forward the Eastern Winds Ruby gear will be swapped out for something different, potentially an emote or a title. Let me know what you think or give me a or on this post. (edited)
11
6
jjgaymer☠
Hey @Partners @Affiliate , quick update on the Drops reformat we talked about before. After digging into it, unfortunately the proposed setup is not possible for a couple of reasons. Twitch does not allow us to switch a campaign from partners only to everyone mid run without fully stopping it. Some of you flagged that earlier and you were right. If we did stop it, we would need to create new voyages, gold, and renown for both campaigns which would be a huge extra lift. Because we also cannot overlap two campaigns, the original format just is not workable. The only approach left is the version I posted below. Partners get an exclusive item plus two other rewards, while everyone else gets five rewards, all of which have been available in the past in some way. I used the last Drops set as an example, but moving forward the Eastern Winds Ruby gear will be swapped out for something different, potentially an emote or a title. Let me know what you think or give me a or on this post. (edited)

jjgaymer☠ 6/10/2025 12:59 PM
I just wanted to take a moment to talk a bit about some of the restrictions and realities we’re working with when it comes to Twitch Drops. I know there’s often loads of great ideas shared, things like adding more new items, expanding sets, mixing it up more, and I completely get the enthusiasm behind them. Believe me, if I could implement half of the things you’ve suggested, I absolutely would. That said, there are some pretty firm limits in place around what we can and can’t do. I’ve asked all the questions, poked at all the edges, and these are the boundaries that exist right now, for better or worse: • We can only include one new cosmetic per Drop, full stop. That’s our cap. • We can include older “vault” items such as the Eastern Winds Ruby, Gilded Phoenix, or Twilight Hunter pieces to help round things out. Sometimes when we have items from bigger campaigns like the 40th Anniversary, we’re able to work those in too if we’re given them, like we did with the Eastern Winds Ruby items. • Once the Amethyst clothing set has fully run its course, the plan is to shift towards individual cosmetics rather than multi item sets, so you won’t have to wait months to complete anything. • We still have full flexibility to include things like Voyages, Renown, and Gold, so those will continue to help balance each Drop. • And finally, there’s the ongoing conversation about balancing Partner exclusive Drops with Everyone Drops, which we’re trying to keep fair and rewarding for both groups.
3
12:59 PM
So while I completely understand the desire for bigger or flashier Drops or multiple new cosmetics each time, that just isn’t something we can achieve under the current setup. My focus has to be on working creatively within those limits to deliver the best experience we can for Partners and the wider community. I just wanted to make sure everyone has a clear picture of where those boundaries are and why certain ideas, even really good ones, might not be possible right now. Appreciate all the feedback, ideas, and patience as always
3
TheCrimsonCrafter [AIU] 6/10/2025 1:04 PM

I know there’s often loads of great ideas shared, things like adding more new items, expanding sets, mixing it up more, and I completely get the enthusiasm behind them. Believe me, if I could implement half of the things you’ve suggested, I absolutely would. That said, there are some pretty firm limits in place around what we can and can’t do. I’ve asked all the questions, poked at all the edges, and these are the boundaries that exist right now, for better or worse: • We can only include one new cosmetic per Drop, full stop. That’s our cap. • We can include older “vault” items such as the Eastern Winds Ruby, Gilded Phoenix, or Twilight Hunter pieces to help round things out. Sometimes when we have items from bigger campaigns like the 40th Anniversary, we’re able to work those in too if we’re given them, like we did with the Eastern Winds Ruby items. • Once the Amethyst clothing set has fully run its course, the plan is to shift towards individual cosmetics rather than multi item sets, so you won’t have to wait months to complete anything. • We still have full flexibility to include things like Voyages, Renown, and Gold, so those will continue to help balance each Drop. • And finally, there’s the ongoing conversation about balancing Partner exclusive Drops with Everyone Drops, which we’re trying to keep fair and rewarding for both groups.

Somes games get seasonal quests or challenges that unlock progressively as the season goes on so it gives exp boosts during the season but also allows people that are behind to do them all at once to catch up if they didn't have time beforehand

HitboTC 5/11/2025 7:41 PM
I understand the point, but this isn’t anything unusual. Many games run Support-a-Streamer or subscription-based cosmetic events. It’s a standard industry practice at this point. And last year’s campaign didn’t just “do okay” — it had a major positive impact. It boosted streamers across the board, not just a few big names. It brought new viewers into communities and genuinely helped creators continue what they do. That’s a tangible benefit, not just a cosmetic lock. So while I get that not everyone loves cosmetics tied to subs, it’s important to look at the actual results. It was beneficial for the health of the creator community which in turn helps the game.
HitboTC
I understand the point, but this isn’t anything unusual. Many games run Support-a-Streamer or subscription-based cosmetic events. It’s a standard industry practice at this point. And last year’s campaign didn’t just “do okay” — it had a major positive impact. It boosted streamers across the board, not just a few big names. It brought new viewers into communities and genuinely helped creators continue what they do. That’s a tangible benefit, not just a cosmetic lock. So while I get that not everyone loves cosmetics tied to subs, it’s important to look at the actual results. It was beneficial for the health of the creator community which in turn helps the game.
Sayuta 5/11/2025 8:26 PM
Oh I'm not saying it's bad it's a good way to support streamers But it will be more badly received for Sea of Thieves specifically because it has more younger players and they don't really have the money for it as opposed to other games like Arc Raiders that has a more mature audience and it's just a no brainer to get something out of supporting a streamer (edited)
Sayuta
Oh I'm not saying it's bad it's a good way to support streamers But it will be more badly received for Sea of Thieves specifically because it has more younger players and they don't really have the money for it as opposed to other games like Arc Raiders that has a more mature audience and it's just a no brainer to get something out of supporting a streamer (edited)
HitboTC 5/11/2025 8:40 PM
I understand the concern, but I genuinely do not think that is how it will play out. Last year's Support-a-Streamer campaign was overwhelmingly positive. People were excited to support their favorite creators, and the Obsidian Capstan ended up being one of the most talked about cosmetics of the year. I have heard nothing but positive feedback from that event. The idea that the Sea of Thieves playerbase will react poorly because it "skews younger" does not really match what we saw. People who wanted the item found a way to support the creator they enjoyed. The community did not struggle with it. They leaned in and participated. So while the concern makes sense in theory, the reality is that the Sea of Thieves community has already shown it will support campaigns like this when they provide value to both creators and players. This is consistent with what many other games do, and last year's results speak for themselves.
Sayuta 5/11/2025 10:09 PM
The difference with the obsidian capstan is that it had multiple ways to earn it Subscribing wasn't the only way to earn it So you suggest making the item available in multiple places would work better? (edited)

What these games tend to do, is offer the cosmetic as a limited exclusivity. Get it now before everyone else before it becomes available in game in some way.

I’ll be honest, this shouldn’t be about those of us already making sustainable money. It’s about the stories from smaller streamers who are trying to make it, who’ve turned a hobby into income that puts food on the table in ways they never thought possible before. That’s where the real passion lives. I’ve been hearing this more and more throughout my community, and it genuinely makes me very happy. I'm not an Xmas person by default, but stuff like this makes me smile.

1:13 PM
I used to be Albion Online partner and they ruined their entire drops viewership by doing too many drops please stick it to one week only, except special events, and leave CW drops to 2 days.
NateaCuiki 19/01/2026 2:25 PM
Previously, exclusive Partner Drops lasted 3 days. Why not do it that way again?
  • Partner Drops - From Friday at 11:00 UTC until Monday at 11:00 UTC. That's it.
  • Community Weekend Drops - They should last exactly the same duration as the event: the event starts, the Drops start, and when the event ends, the Drops stop. There's no reason to extend it for another week. And honestly, I'd just put the good Obsidian Drop in the drop slots during the event, and that's it. The purpose is to have fun during the event and do the activities during that period.
  • I would only have 1-week Drops for the Act 1 Everyone Drops during the new season's launch week.
And of course, Partner Drops should also be available during the first weekend of the Act 2 and 3 launch. Years ago, we always did this, and it worked perfectly. I don't know why it's so difficult to do now. (edited)

Captain Falcore 23/01/2026 11:11 PM
Can we make it a New Year’s resolution for 2026 to have just one event or Twitch Drops campaign where all the details are correct across the board. Times, dates, participants, and items are accurate in every social post, every website link, and every video promoting the event. I’m being honest here, but throughout 2024 and 2025, almost every event, not just Twitch Drops but also in game limited events and other promotional material, has had some kind of mistake. Whether it was the wrong time, wrong item, wrong date, or wrong participants, something has always been off. I’m about as organised as an ashtray on a motorcycle, and even I have never missed a beat like this.
